what_it_does: Implements the deterministic, AST-based structural half of a coherence substrate: it extracts API routes from the runtime bridge, checks whether they are actually called by frontend or test code, compares node-type files on disk against a live registry, proposes candidate dead capabilities and hardcoded vocabularies, and manages the lifecycle of findings (reconcile, disposition, burn-down, signoff) against an append-only store.
observations: Route extraction uses ast.Compare to find routing decisions and unions a regex fallback so missed forms are added, never removed.
Consumer detection strips comments and existence-assertions, then requires a real HTTP call marker on the same line as the route.
Registry-vs-live is an exact set difference between node filenames with a run() function and the supplied live_types set.
Capability-no-consumer and hardcoding detectors are explicitly candidate-only and never auto-acted.
Findings are deduplicated by (kind, address) and folded at read time over an append-only store.
Dispositions include agent-settable values and an operator-only 'by-design' that requires confirmed=True.
structural_signoff is designed as a hard boolean gate for convergence merges, failing loud if live_types is not provided.
The file references an orphan-routes catalogue at design/_system/orphan-routes.json to seed accepted dispositions.
standouts: The safe-direction discipline: static analysis is allowed to over-call dead (false orphan) but must never declare a dead route wired (false wire).
The operator-only 'by-design' disposition creates a consent floor for permanently accepting a gap.
Separation of trustworthy structural findings (burn-down must-fix) from fuzzy candidate findings (adjudicate only).
conventions: Heavy explanatory docstrings with rationale and cross-references to research disciplines.
Uppercase module-level constants for regexes and disposition tuples.
Fail-loud: reports 'not-run' instead of silently passing when live_types is missing.
concerns: File reads use errors='ignore' and no context managers, risking leaked handles and silent corruption.
_strip_comments is regex-based and could mangle route literals inside strings that happen to contain // or #.
route_is_wired requires the call marker and route on the same line, missing multiline or chained calls.
Consumer detection does not handle f-strings, template literals, or URL variables.
hardcoding_candidates only scans runtime/*.py, missing other modules that might contain registry-like literals.
notes: An AST-grounded, model-free structural coherence gate that detects unwired routes, registry drift, and candidate dead code, then folds findings into a disposition-aware burn-down.
questions: What is the exact store interface (append_finding, disposition_for, all_findings, append_disposition)?
How is live_types obtained from capabilities().node_types in practice?
What is the schema of design/_system/orphan-routes.json?
What is the '4B swarm' semantic layer and how does it adjudicate candidates?
What does RHM verb whitelist refer to?
How are candidate findings surfaced to an operator or model for adjudication?
purpose_vs_actual: Stated purpose matches actual content: it is the exact, deterministic, model-free structural half of coherence, with separate candidate-only detectors and a finding/disposition lifecycle.
apparent_themes: Separation of trustworthy structural vs fuzzy semantic adjudication
Consent and escalation for accepting gaps
intention_signals: To build an auditable, hard gate that prevents convergence merges when built structural code lacks callers or drifts from the live registry, while keeping heuristic or model-based judgments in a separate adjudication lane.
novelty: The explicit false-orphan vs false-wire safety discipline, the union-fallback route extractor, the operator-only disposition floor, and the structural signoff as a merge-blocking boolean.
contribution: A deterministic, AST-grounded detector suite and finding-lifecycle model (reconcile, disposition, burn-down, signoff) that can feed a convergence gate without invoking a model, while clearly separating must-fix structural findings from propose-only candidate findings.
summary_for_embedding: company/runtime/coherence_detect.py implements AST-grounded, model-free structural coherence detectors for API route reachability, node registry drift, dead-capability candidates, and hardcoded-literal candidates, plus an append-only finding store lifecycle (reconcile, disposition policy, burn-down, signoff).
intention_for_embedding: Provide a trustworthy structural half of a coherence substrate that can block convergence merges when built code lacks callers or registry drift exists, while keeping fuzzy adjudication separate.

symbols.does: AST-extracts /api/ route literals used in routing decisions, with a regex fallback union.
symbols.description: Parses bridge source to find string constants compared against self.path, then unions regex-discovered route literals so unusual routing forms are still counted, never shrunk.

symbols.does: Collects all string constants from an AST expression node, including tuple/list/set elements.
symbols.description: Recursive helper that returns every string Constant inside a node, enabling route extraction from both single comparisons and membership tests like self.path in (...).

symbols.does: Removes JS/TS block comments and line comments (// and #) to eliminate false-wire mentions.
symbols.description: Best-effort comment stripping that deletes block comments and truncates lines at // or #, so route literals inside comments are not mistaken for real callers.

symbols.does: Returns true if a route appears on a comment-stripped line that also carries an HTTP call marker and is not an existence assertion.
symbols.description: Positive-only consumer check that requires a call marker (fetch, EventSource, requests, axios, etc.) on the same line as the route, excluding existence-assertion lines to avoid false wires.

symbols.does: Reads bridge, frontend, and test files to return all routes and a wired flag per route.
symbols.description: Orchestrates route extraction and consumer detection across the runtime bridge, canvas TypeScript sources, and Python tests (excluding meta acceptance files).

symbols.does: Compares node-type files on disk against the live registry set.
symbols.description: Exact set-difference detector that reports node stems with run() on disk but not in live_types, and live types with no corresponding file, treating either direction as drift.

symbols.does: Proposes Suite public methods with no reachable caller from faces or tests.
symbols.description: Builds an internal call graph of Suite methods, seeds reachability from callers found in bridge, MCP face, implement, cognition, and tests, and returns public methods not reached; explicitly candidate-only because dynamic dispatch is invisible to AST.

symbols.does: Returns the stable (kind, address) tuple used to deduplicate findings.
symbols.description: Simple identity function that gives each finding a persistent handle for upsert, reconcile, and burn-down folding.

symbols.does: Computes known, new, and resolved findings between two handle sets.
symbols.description: Universal upsert logic: current ∩ prior are known, current − prior are fresh gaps, prior − current are auto-resolved; also reports net_change as a thrash signal.

symbols.does: Sets a finding disposition under a consent policy.
symbols.description: Validates disposition, escalates operator-only 'by-design' unless confirmed, and otherwise records the disposition in the store; fails loud for unknown dispositions.

symbols.does: Returns a hard pass/fail for convergence merge gating based on structural evidence.
symbols.description: Checks for new uncatalogued unwired routes and registry-vs-live drift; reports 'not-run' rather than silently passing when live_types is missing, producing a boolean that can block merge.

symbols.does: On-demand coherence read that records structural findings and returns burn-down plus candidates.
symbols.description: Creates a temporary store if none is provided, writes structural findings, and returns the burn-down plus candidate-only reports for capability-no-consumer and hardcoding.

symbols.does: Renders a scan result into a scannable CLI string.
symbols.description: Formats the burn-down headline, open backlog by disposition, accepted dispositions, kind counts, and candidate-only findings in clearly separated sections.

symbols.does: Runs route reachability and writes unwired-route findings into the store.
symbols.description: Records each unwired route as a structural finding and seeds dispositions from the orphan-routes catalogue only when no prior disposition exists, preserving operator decisions across re-detections.

symbols.does: Read-time fold over the finding store joined with dispositions.
symbols.description: Dedups append-only findings by handle, classifies each as open, accepted, or closed based on disposition, and returns counts plus the open backlog.

symbols.does: Proposes large module-level uppercase literals in runtime/*.py that may be hidden registries.
symbols.description: AST scan for UPPER_CASE assignments of dict/list/tuple/set with at least min_entries; candidate-only because some large literals are legitimate constants or grammar sources.
